text:
MCP tool descriptions are the only part of an API contract that's written for machines but reviewed by humans.

humans think a 500-word description is thorough. machines think it's expensive.

this is why schema quality is an engineering discipline, not a style preference.

we have eslint for code. sqlfluff for SQL. hadolint for dockerfiles.

we don't have a standard linter for tool descriptions — the part of the stack that sits between your code and the language model.

agent-friend is that linter. pip install agent-friend

the arxiv paper measuring MCP servers found 97.1% of tools have at least one quality smell.

we found the same across 201 servers: 100% have at least one issue.

not because developers are careless. because there were no standards to check against.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
